title: podman logs -f: hangs forever as nonroot
body:
```
$ podman run --rm -d --name c1 alpine sh -c 'echo a;sleep 10;echo b'
9151e85b082d578aaa8cd771518cb6ed54c8b2ded72471edf166490eefcabd85
$ podman logs -f c1
a
b
```
This now hangs forever (podman @ master). Works as expected (exits when container exits) when run as root.

Caught by BATS tests.

comments.body: Rough timeframe on when this broke? Between the last release that went into 8 and now?
I'm guessing the last 2 days; I'm pretty sure I ran my bats tests on master on Tuesday. But it might've been Monday.
Alright, definitely not the changes I was thinking, then...
git bisect blames: 629017bb197bcda31ac129fb712ba3c6917b100b , and my manual testing confirms, but that makes no sense....

Uh... huh...
Alright that one's strange.

that commit has broken many other things with rootless containers.  If you try your command twice, you'll see it will fail twice as c1 is not removed despite `--rm` being passed.

https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3328 is fixing this issue too

@giuseppe mind adding some integration tests for some of those things that were broken?  it might help us in the future? if time is a concern, then lets list what they should be and between the interns and I, maybe we can write them?
this issue could be easily detected with the test I've added here: https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3347
